evals.name: b2b-saas-tool
evals.prompt: Create a cold email strategy for PostPilot, a B2B SaaS tool that automates social media scheduling and analytics for marketing agencies. Pricing: $149/mo starter, $349/mo growth. Founded by Sarah Chen. Website: postpilot.io. They have a case study where a 12-person agency went from posting 3x/week to daily across 8 client accounts, saving 15 hours/week. Competitors: Hootsuite, Buffer, Sprout Social, Later. CTA: Book a demo. Sequencer: Email Bison. Sender: Sarah Chen, Founder.
evals.expected_output: Complete 4-section strategy document with discovery summary, ICP, offer positioning, and 3 messaging angles targeting marketing agency owners/founders
expectations: Output contains all 4 sections: Discovery, ICP, Offer Positioning, Messaging Strategy
ICP targets MARKETING AGENCIES (the customer), not 'SaaS' or 'Software' companies (the product's industry)
ICP company keywords describe the customer's business (e.g., 'marketing agency', 'social media management', 'digital marketing') NOT the product's features
ICP includes job titles with buying authority (Founder, CEO, Owner, Director-level)
ICP includes negative filters to exclude competitors and irrelevant companies
Pain points are specific and surgical, not generic (reference specific agency pain like juggling client accounts, missed posting schedules, time spent on scheduling)
AI qualification prompt is present and copy-paste ready with placeholder variables
Offer positioning identifies a clear value lever (save time, save money, or make money)
3 distinct messaging angles present (Pain Dagger, Proof Machine, Value Gift) that are genuinely different approaches, not reworded versions of the same pitch
Tone profile includes formality scale (1-5), personality traits, and banned words
Front-end offer or lead magnet strategy is defined
Implicit objections mapped with counters (at least 3)
Output references Email Bison as the sequencer
No AI slop words (delve, leverage, synergy, game-changer, revolutionize, cutting-edge)

evals.name: local-service-business
evals.prompt: Create a cold email strategy for TexasComfort HVAC, a commercial HVAC maintenance and installation company in Houston, TX. They specialize in preventive maintenance contracts for office buildings, retail spaces, and warehouses. Pricing: maintenance contracts start at $500/mo per building. They have 18 years experience, 200+ commercial buildings under contract, and a 98% emergency response rate within 4 hours. Competitors: local HVAC contractors, national chains like Comfort Systems USA. CTA: Book a building walkthrough. Sequencer: Instantly. Senders: Mike Torres (Owner), Lisa Park (Account Manager).
evals.expected_output: Complete 4-section strategy targeting commercial property managers and facility directors in Houston metro area
expectations: Output contains all 4 sections: Discovery, ICP, Offer Positioning, Messaging Strategy
ICP targets PROPERTY MANAGERS and FACILITY DIRECTORS (the customers), not 'HVAC' companies
ICP geography is specific to Houston/Texas metro area
ICP company size is appropriate for commercial property management (not targeting enterprise or solopreneur)
Pain points reference HVAC-specific problems (equipment downtime, tenant complaints, energy costs, emergency repairs, compliance)
Pain points are phrased as acute questions that make the prospect feel understood, not generic marketing questions
Offer positioning uses the 'So What?' framework to reach human outcomes, not just feature lists
Front-end offer leverages the free building walkthrough CTA effectively
3 messaging angles are genuinely different entry points into the same offer
Tone profile matches a local service business (not overly corporate, not too casual)
Output references Instantly as the sequencer (not Email Bison)
Case study data (200+ buildings, 98% response rate, 18 years) is woven into proof elements, not ignored

evals.name: professional-services-high-ticket
evals.prompt: Create a cold email strategy for Meridian CFO Partners, a fractional CFO firm targeting Series A and Series B SaaS startups. They provide financial modeling, fundraising support, board reporting, and cash runway management. Pricing: $8,000-$15,000/mo retainer. Case study: helped a Series A fintech extend their runway by 9 months through cost optimization, then led their Series B raise ($18M). Competitors: Pilot, Bench (lower-end), Big 4 advisory (higher-end), in-house hire. CTA: 30-minute strategy call. Sequencer: Email Bison. Sender: David Park, Managing Partner.
evals.expected_output: Complete 4-section strategy targeting SaaS startup founders and C-suite with executive-appropriate tone
expectations: Output contains all 4 sections: Discovery, ICP, Offer Positioning, Messaging Strategy
ICP targets STARTUP FOUNDERS and C-SUITE at SaaS companies (CEO, COO, VP Finance), not 'financial services' or 'accounting' companies
ICP company size targets funded startups (Series A/B stage, likely 20-150 employees)
ICP includes buying triggers relevant to startups (recent funding round, hiring finance roles, board meeting prep)
Pain points are specific to funded startup pain (burn rate anxiety, investor reporting pressure, not having a finance leader, fundraising prep)
Offer positioning handles the high price point appropriately — positions against the cost of a full-time CFO hire ($200K+), not against cheaper bookkeeping services
Implicit objections address the 'we can't afford $8K-15K/mo' concern with ROI framing
Tone profile is appropriately formal (3-4 on 1-5 scale) for executive audience
Messaging angles differentiate between pain-driven, proof-driven, and value-driven approaches
The $18M Series B case study is featured prominently in the Proof Machine angle
No AI slop words or overly salesy language inappropriate for executive audience

evals.name: edge-case-minimal-context
evals.prompt: Create a cold email strategy for my company.
evals.expected_output: The skill should NOT generate a strategy with made-up details. It should ask discovery questions to gather the required information.
expectations: Does NOT generate a full strategy with fabricated company details
Asks discovery questions about the company, offer, target customer, pricing, case studies
References the discovery interview framework (what info is needed and why)
Asks about CTA strategy (book a call vs lead magnet)
Asks about sequencer preference (Email Bison or Instantly)
Asks about sender personas
Offers to process materials (transcripts, docs, recordings) if available
Tone is helpful and structured, not overwhelming with too many questions at once

evals.name: edge-case-transcript-ingestion
evals.prompt:
Create a cold email strategy for BrightPath Recruiting. Here's a transcript from a sales call:

SARAH (Founder): So what we do is, we place software engineers — specifically full-stack and backend devs — for mid-size tech companies. Not the Googles and Metas, but the Series B through D companies that are scaling their engineering teams.

PROSPECT: How do you differ from the hundred other recruiters in my inbox?

SARAH: Fair question. Two things. First, we only work with engineers who've passed our technical screen — it's a 3-hour assessment that mirrors real work. So you're not wasting time interviewing people who can't code. Second, we guarantee a 90-day retention. If they leave or you fire them in 90 days, we replace them free.

PROSPECT: What does it cost?

SARAH: 20% of first-year salary. Industry standard is 20-25%, so we're competitive. But the real savings is in time — our average time-to-fill is 18 days vs industry average of 42.

PROSPECT: We've been burned by recruiters sending us unqualified candidates.

SARAH: Yeah, that's exactly why we built the technical screen. Last quarter we placed 47 engineers, and our client satisfaction score was 94%.

Sequencer: Email Bison. Sender: Sarah Mitchell, Founder.
evals.expected_output: Strategy that extracts key details from the transcript (technical screen, 90-day guarantee, 18-day fill time, 47 placements, 94% satisfaction) and uses founder's own language
expectations: Extracts company details from transcript without asking for them again (recruiting firm, software engineers, Series B-D companies)
Uses specific metrics from transcript: 3-hour technical screen, 90-day guarantee, 18-day time-to-fill, 47 placements, 94% satisfaction
Preserves founder's voice and language patterns from the transcript
ICP targets VP of Engineering, CTO, Head of Engineering at mid-size tech companies (Series B-D)
Pain points reference the specific objection from the transcript (unqualified candidates from other recruiters)
Identifies what's still MISSING after transcript analysis and asks targeted follow-up questions (not a full interview)
Offer positioning leverages the 90-day guarantee and technical screen as key differentiators
Does not fabricate information not present in the transcript
